demos.question: Konnichiwa was launched in a broadcast that was streamed by the platform that was founded in what city in 2010?
demos.trajectory:
[[ ## thought_0 ## ]]
I need to find out which platform was founded in 2010 and in which city. This information will help me answer the question about the broadcast of Konnichiwa. I will search for platforms that were established in 2010 to identify the correct one.

[[ ## tool_name_0 ## ]]
search_wikipedia

[[ ## tool_args_0 ## ]]
{"query": "platform founded in 2010"}

[[ ## observation_0 ## ]]
[1] «Genius Crowds | Genius Crowds was a crowdsourcing platform founded in 2010 on the principles of collaboration and sharing. It served as a platform for everyday people – students, women, men, moms, dads, DIY'ers, cooks, and tinkerers - who have a product idea that they don't know how to bring to market.»
[2] «Seedups | SeedUps is an equity crowdfunding platform for seed investment to launch and focuses on startup companies. It was founded by Michael Faulkner and Gavin Gallagher in 2010 and is aimed at tech startups in the USA, Ireland and the UK. Since launch over 2200 tech businesses have registered. It is only available to accredited or sophisticated investors. The platform is headquartered in Republic of Ireland with offices in the UK, US and Canada. Each funding project on SeedUps lasts for the same duration, namely 180 days.»
[3] «Sponsume | Sponsume was an online multicurrency crowd funding platform founded by French entrepreneur Gregory Vincent in 2010. Its headquarters are in London, United Kingdom.»

[[ ## thought_1 ## ]]
I found several platforms founded in 2010, including Seedups and Sponsume. Seedups is headquartered in the Republic of Ireland, while Sponsume is based in London, UK. I need to determine which of these platforms is related to the broadcast of Konnichiwa. I will search for more specific information about Konnichiwa to see if it is linked to either of these platforms.

[[ ## tool_name_1 ## ]]
search_wikipedia

[[ ## tool_args_1 ## ]]
{"query": "Konnichiwa broadcast platform"}

[[ ## observation_1 ## ]]
[1] «Konnichiwa (Skepta album) | Konnichiwa is the fourth studio album by British grime artist Skepta. After numerous delays, it was released on 6 May 2016 by Boy Better Know. The album was launched with a party in Tokyo on 5 May 2016 arranged and broadcast globally by live streaming platform Boiler Room, featuring Skepta performing the whole album live with supporting performances from Japanese artists Kohh, Dutch Montana, Loota, and DJ Riki.»
[2] «Konnichiwa (Youmou & Ohana album) | Konnichiwa (Japanese: こんにちは。) is a 2008 album by Youmou & Ohana. The first track, "Falling", features the music of Corinne Bailey Rae.»
[3] «Konnichiwa (The Superions song) | "Konnichiwa" is the seventh single by The Superions, a side project of Fred Schneider of The B-52s. The single was released to iTunes Stores as a digital download on May 12, 2014 and is the first single to be released from a collection titled "The Vertical Mind".»

[[ ## thought_2 ## ]]
I found that the Konnichiwa broadcast was linked to the live streaming platform Boiler Room. However, I need to confirm the founding details of Boiler Room to see if it was established in 2010 and in which city. I will search for information about Boiler Room's founding.

[[ ## tool_name_2 ## ]]
search_wikipedia

[[ ## tool_args_2 ## ]]
{"query": "Boiler Room founding details"}

[[ ## observation_2 ## ]]
[1] «Boiler Room (music project) | Boiler Room is a global online music broadcasting platform commissioning and streaming live music sessions around the world. Founded in London, England in 2010, Boiler Room has now hosted shows in around 100 cities worldwide, from Stockholm to Shanghai. They have regular operations in London, New York City, Berlin, Mexico City, Sydney, São Paulo, Tokyo, Kraków, and Los Angeles and produce an average of 30–35 new shows each month. Their music programming originally focused on electronic music such as garage, house, techno, dub but quickly expanded to include additional genres, including grime, hip hop, classical, and jazz.»
[2] «Boiler Room (band) | Boiler Room was a nu metal band who formed in 1996. The band, featuring vocalist Chris Lino, guitarist Rob Caggiano, bassist James Meselsohn, and drummer Mike Meselsohn toured through their local club scene for several years, building up their material and performance skills. The band broke through on local radio following an opening slot for Orgy in 1999. Original guitarist Joey Barracato played on their first self-titled 1996 CD and played the first couple years of gigs, opening for bands such as Type O Negative, Life of Agony, and Korn.»
[3] «Joakim Borda-Pedreira | Joakim Borda (born Joakim Waskar Borda-Pedreira 1977 in Malmö) is a Swedish-Bolivian art historian, art critic and curator. He has been Art Editor of Plaza Magazine (2007-2011) and is the Founding Director of The Boiler Room Gallery in Oslo. Between 2012-2015 he was Head Curator of The Arts Festival of North Norway. Borda is grandson of the Bolivian poet and politician Hector Borda Leaño.»
demos.next_thought: I have confirmed that Boiler Room was founded in London, England in 2010. Since Konnichiwa was launched with a party arranged and broadcast globally by Boiler Room, I now have all the information needed to answer the question.

demos.question: In addition to Ryan Gosling, Angourie Rice and the actor born on October 11, 1977, who else starred in The Nice Guys?
Which publication has been published by a greater number of groups, House Beautiful or Smithsonian?
In what year was the creator of the character that appeared in a series of films directed by Stephen Norrington, Guillermo del Toro and Goyer born?

signature.instructions:
You are a knowledgeable trivia agent. Given the fields `question`, produce the fields `answer`. In each episode, you will receive the `question` as input along with a `trajectory` that tracks your previous thoughts and tool calls. Your goal is to utilize the supplied tools to gather necessary information for producing the `answer`. 

Interleave your reasoning in `next_thought`, select the appropriate `next_tool_name` (either `search_wikipedia` to gather more information or `finish` to complete the task), and provide the necessary `next_tool_args`. After each tool call, append the resulting observation to your trajectory. 

Reason about the current situation and plan your next steps as you work through the task. The tools available to you are:
(1) `search_wikipedia`, which takes arguments {'query': {'type': 'string'}} in JSON format.
(2) `finish`, which signals that all information for producing the outputs, i.e. `answer`, is now available.

demos.question: That Darn Cat! and Never a Dull Moment were both produced by what studio?
demos.trajectory:
[[ ## thought_0 ## ]]
I need to find out which studio produced both "That Darn Cat!" and "Never a Dull Moment." This information is likely available on Wikipedia, so I will search for it there.

[[ ## tool_name_0 ## ]]
search_wikipedia

[[ ## tool_args_0 ## ]]
{"query": "That Darn Cat! and Never a Dull Moment studio production"}

[[ ## observation_0 ## ]]
[1] «That Darn Cat! | That Darn Cat! is a 1965 American Walt Disney Productions thriller comedy film starring Hayley Mills (in her last of the six films she made for the Walt Disney Studios) and Dean Jones (starring in his first film for Disney) in a story about bank robbers, a kidnapping and a mischievous cat. The film was based on the 1963 novel "Undercover Cat" by Gordon and Mildred Gordon and was directed by Robert Stevenson. The title song was written by the Sherman Brothers and sung by Bobby Darin. The 1997 remake includes a cameo appearance by Dean Jones.»
[2] «Never a Dull Moment (1968 film) | Never a Dull Moment is a 1968 American comedy crime film from Walt Disney Productions starring Dick Van Dyke and Edward G. Robinson and directed by Jerry Paris. The script by AJ Carothers was based on a novel by John Godey. The supporting cast features Dorothy Provine, Henry Silva, Slim Pickens and Jack Elam.»
[3] «That Darn Cat (1997 film) | That Darn Cat is a 1997 American mystery comedy film starring Christina Ricci and Doug E. Doug. It is a remake of the 1965 film "That Darn Cat! ", which in turn was based on the book "Undercover Cat" by Gordon and Mildred Gordon. It is directed by British TV veteran Bob Spiers (most famous for "Fawlty Towers", as well as "Spice World") and written by Scott Alexander and Larry Karaszewski, best known for "Ed Wood" and the first two "Problem Child" films.»

[[ ## thought_1 ## ]]
Both "That Darn Cat!" and "Never a Dull Moment" were produced by Walt Disney Productions. I have the information I need to answer the question.

[[ ## tool_name_1 ## ]]
finish

[[ ## tool_args_1 ## ]]
{}

[[ ## observation_1 ## ]]
Completed.
demos.reasoning: Both "That Darn Cat!" and "Never a Dull Moment" were produced by Walt Disney Productions, as confirmed by the information retrieved from Wikipedia.

[2] «The Strange Death of Liberal England | The Strange Death of Liberal England is a book written by George Dangerfield published in 1935. In it the author discusses the causes of the decline in the influence of the British Liberal Party in the years 1910 to 1914.»
[3] «The Strange Death of Tory England | The Strange Death of Tory England is a book of political commentary by the journalist Geoffrey Wheatcroft, published in 2005.»

[[ ## thought_1 ## ]]

[[ ## tool_name_1 ## ]]
search_wikipedia

[[ ## tool_args_1 ## ]]
{"query": "Douglas Murray founded Centre"}

[[ ## observation_1 ## ]]
[1] «Douglas Murray (author) | Douglas Kear Murray (born 16 July 1979) is a British author, journalist, and political commentator. He is the founder of the Centre for Social Cohesion and is currently the associate director of the Henry Jackson Society and associate editor of "The Spectator".»
[2] «Big Switch Networks | Big Switch Networks is a data center networking company. The company was founded by Kyle Forster and Guido Appenzeller in 2010 and has roots in the original Stanford research team that invented software defined networking (SDN). Douglas Murray, a former Juniper Networks and Sun Microsystems executive, joined the company as CEO in 2013 as the company went through a significant pivot following VMware's acquisition of Nicira.»
[3] «Westgate, Oxford | The Westgate Shopping Centre (locally known as the Westgate Centre or Westgate) is a shopping centre in central Oxford, England, currently undergoing redevelopment. The original centre was built between 1970–72, designed by Douglas Murray and built by Taylor Woodrow. The centre was closed in February 2016 for comprehensive redevelopment, and is planned to re-open in October 2017.»

[[ ## thought_2 ## ]]
I found that Douglas Murray is the founder of the Centre for Social Cohesion. This answers the question about which Centre he founded. I can now finish the task.

finish

[[ ## tool_args_2 ## ]]

Completed.
demos.reasoning: Douglas Murray is the founder of the Centre for Social Cohesion, which focuses on issues related to social integration and cohesion in the UK. This information was confirmed through a search that highlighted his role as a political commentator and author.

demos.question: Which publication has been published by a greater number of groups, House Beautiful or Smithsonian?
demos.trajectory:
[[ ## thought_0 ## ]]
I need to find out how many groups have published the magazine House Beautiful and how many have published Smithsonian. This information is likely available on Wikipedia, so I will search for both publications to compare their publication histories.

[[ ## tool_name_0 ## ]]
search_wikipedia

[[ ## tool_args_0 ## ]]
{"query": "House Beautiful magazine publication history"}

[[ ## observation_0 ## ]]
[1] «House Beautiful | House Beautiful is an interior decorating magazine that focuses on decorating and the domestic arts. First published in 1896, it is currently published by the Hearst Corporation, who began publishing it in 1934. It is the oldest still-published magazine in what is known as the "shelter magazine" genre.»
[2] «Ethel B. Power | Ethel B. Power (1881–1969) was the editor of "House Beautiful" magazine in the 1920s and early 1930s, and in this capacity she championed a generation of women architects who emerged from the newly founded Cambridge School of Architecture and Landscape Architecture. A trained architect herself, she also published a compilation of plans for small houses by notable architects of the day.»
[3] «Elizabeth Gordon (editor) | Elizabeth Gordon (August 8, 1906 - September 3, 2000) was editor of House Beautiful magazine from 1941 to 1964. She was remembered as "a missionary of taste to the American homemaker."»

[[ ## thought_1 ## ]]
I have found some information about House Beautiful, but I need to search for the publication history of Smithsonian to compare the number of groups that have published each magazine.

[[ ## tool_name_1 ## ]]
search_wikipedia

[[ ## tool_args_1 ## ]]
{"query": "Smithsonian magazine publication history"}

[[ ## observation_1 ## ]]
[1] «Smithsonian (magazine) | Smithsonian is the official journal published by the Smithsonian Institution in Washington, D.C. The first issue was published in 1970.»
[2] «Air & Space/Smithsonian | Air & Space/Smithsonian magazine is a bimonthly magazine put out by the National Air and Space Museum. Its first publishing was in April 1986. Because the museum is a part of the Smithsonian Institution, which puts out its own "Smithsonian" magazine, the magazine's full title is "Air & Space/Smithsonian". Therefore, the word "Smithsonian" appears on the cover, just beneath "AIR&SPACE", in a much smaller size.»
[3] «Smithsonian Contributions and Studies Series | The Smithsonian Contributions and Studies Series is a collection of serial periodical publications produced by the Smithsonian Institution, detailing advances in various scientific and societal fields to which the Smithsonian Institution has made contributions.»

[[ ## thought_2 ## ]]
I have found that House Beautiful has been published since 1896 and is currently published by the Hearst Corporation. Smithsonian magazine, on the other hand, was first published in 1970 by the Smithsonian Institution. However, I still need to determine how many different groups have published each magazine over their histories. I will search for more detailed publication history for both magazines to find this information.

[[ ## tool_name_2 ## ]]
search_wikipedia

[[ ## tool_args_2 ## ]]
{"query": "House Beautiful magazine publishers history"}

[[ ## observation_2 ## ]]
[1] «House Beautiful | House Beautiful is an interior decorating magazine that focuses on decorating and the domestic arts. First published in 1896, it is currently published by the Hearst Corporation, who began publishing it in 1934. It is the oldest still-published magazine in what is known as the "shelter magazine" genre.»
[2] «Ethel B. Power | Ethel B. Power (1881–1969) was the editor of "House Beautiful" magazine in the 1920s and early 1930s, and in this capacity she championed a generation of women architects who emerged from the newly founded Cambridge School of Architecture and Landscape Architecture. A trained architect herself, she also published a compilation of plans for small houses by notable architects of the day.»
[3] «Elizabeth Gordon (editor) | Elizabeth Gordon (August 8, 1906 - September 3, 2000) was editor of House Beautiful magazine from 1941 to 1964. She was remembered as "a missionary of taste to the American homemaker."»

[[ ## thought_3 ## ]]
I still need to find out how many different groups have published the Smithsonian magazine. The previous search did not provide enough information about the publishers of Smithsonian. I will search for the publication history of Smithsonian magazine to gather more details.

[[ ## tool_name_3 ## ]]
search_wikipedia

[[ ## tool_args_3 ## ]]
{"query": "Smithsonian magazine publishers history"}

[[ ## observation_3 ## ]]
[1] «Smithsonian (magazine) | Smithsonian is the official journal published by the Smithsonian Institution in Washington, D.C. The first issue was published in 1970.»
[2] «Air & Space/Smithsonian | Air & Space/Smithsonian magazine is a bimonthly magazine put out by the National Air and Space Museum. Its first publishing was in April 1986. Because the museum is a part of the Smithsonian Institution, which puts out its own "Smithsonian" magazine, the magazine's full title is "Air & Space/Smithsonian". Therefore, the word "Smithsonian" appears on the cover, just beneath "AIR&SPACE", in a much smaller size.»
[3] «GOSH! | GOSH! magazine was a Los Angeles-based arts, entertainment, and fashion magazine published in eleven issues between October, 1978 and August, 1979. In its short history it became notable enough to be recognized by the Smithsonian Institution and included in their archives. In addition, GOSH! articles written by Dennis Cooper have been archived as part of the Dennis Cooper Papers in the Fales Library and Special Collections of New York University. It was distributed free of charge in art galleries, alternative bookstores and music shops in the Los Angeles area. Articles ranged from interviews with experimental filmmakers like George Kuchar, Sara Kathryn Arledge, and Ted V. Mikels; influential radio announcers like Rodney Bingenheimer; to reviews of art exhibits, like Susan Greiger's (now Susan Singer) controversial show at Aarnun gallery featuring life-sized nude photos arranged in a flip book and an exhibit about how celebrities and common folk relate to their own noses.»

[[ ## thought_4 ## ]]
I have not yet found the specific number of different groups that have published either House Beautiful or Smithsonian. I need to continue searching for more detailed information about the publishers of both magazines to determine which has been published by a greater number of groups.

search_wikipedia

{"query": "House Beautiful magazine publishers"}

[[ ## observation_4 ## ]]

First published in 1896, it is currently published by the Hearst Corporation, who began publishing it in 1934. It is the oldest still-published magazine in what is known as the "shelter magazine" genre.»
[2] «Ethel B. Power | Ethel B. Power (1881–1969) was the editor of "House Beautiful" magazine in the 1920s and early 1930s, and in this capacity she championed a generation of women architects who emerged from the newly founded Cambridge School of Architecture and Landscape Architecture. A trained architect herself, she also published a compilation of plans for small houses by notable architects of the day.»
[3] «Elizabeth Gordon (editor) | Elizabeth Gordon (August 8, 1906 - September 3, 2000) was editor of House Beautiful magazine from 1941 to 1964. She was remembered as "a missionary of taste to the American homemaker."»
demos.reasoning: House Beautiful has been published since 1896 and is currently published by the Hearst Corporation, which began publishing it in 1934. Smithsonian magazine, on the other hand, was first published in 1970 by the Smithsonian Institution. Given that House Beautiful has a longer history and has been published by at least two different groups (the original publisher and Hearst Corporation), while Smithsonian has been consistently published by the Smithsonian Institution since its inception, it can be concluded that House Beautiful has been published by a greater number of groups.

demos.question: What is the name of the school located near the Brea mall owned by Simon Property Group?
demos.trajectory:
[[ ## thought_0 ## ]]
I need to find the name of the school located near the Brea mall that is owned by Simon Property Group. This information might be available on Wikipedia or other reliable sources. I will search for it to gather the necessary details.

[[ ## tool_name_0 ## ]]
search_wikipedia

[[ ## tool_args_0 ## ]]
{"query": "school near Brea mall owned by Simon Property Group"}

[[ ## observation_0 ## ]]
[1] «Brea Mall | The Brea Mall is a shopping mall located in the Orange County city of Brea, California. Since 1998 the mall has been owned and operated by the Simon Property Group. It is home to four major department stores, over 175 specialty shops and boutiques, and a food court. It is approximately 1,310,000 square feet (1.31 million).»
[2] «Briarwood Mall | Briarwood Mall is a shopping mall in Ann Arbor, Michigan, United States. The mall's four anchor stores are Macy's, JCPenney, Sears, and Von Maur. Surrounded by office and other development, the mall anchors the southern Ann Arbor commercial area around Eisenhower Boulevard and I-94. It serves as the primary shopping mall for all of Washtenaw County. Simon Property Group currently manages and co-owns the mall (Simon owns 50%). Briarwood is considered a Class A mall property by developers based on its sales per square foot. Many restaurants, hotels and stores surround the mall.»
[3] «Mall at The Source | The Mall at the Source (also known as The Source Mall or simply The Source) is a shopping mall located in Westbury, New York, United States, on Old Country Road and Merchants Concourse (Ellison Avenue), currently managed by Newmark Grubb Knight Frank and owned by lenders. Until August 2012, the mall was managed and co-owned by Simon Property Group. Its last stores included an Old Navy, an H&M, and a David's Bridal. It currently only has a couple upscale restaurants — The Cheesecake Factory and P. F. Chang's China Bistro — a Dave & Buster's, a Lord and Taylor outlet, a Fortunoff's outdoor furniture outlet, a Gymboree Play & Music, a few discount retailers, an rescue animal shelter, and small standalone kiosks. The mall was named for its former anchor store Fortunoff (which used the phrase "The Source" in advertising for the chain), which the mall was built around and which closed in June 2009.»

The search did not return any information about the school near the Brea mall owned by Simon Property Group. I need to refine my search or look for other sources that might provide this specific information.

It serves as the primary shopping mall for all of Washtenaw County. Simon Property Group currently manages and co-owns the mall (Simon owns 50%). Briarwood is considered a Class A mall property by developers based on its sales per square foot. Many restaurants, hotels and stores surround the mall.»
[3] «Simon Property Group | Simon Property Group, Inc. is an American commercial real estate company in the United States and the largest real estate investment trust (REIT). It is also the largest shopping mall operator in America.»

It is home to four major department stores, over 175 specialty shops and boutiques, and a food court. It is approximately 1,310,000 square feet (1.31 million).»
[2] «Simon Youth Foundation | Simon Youth Foundation (SYF) is a non-profit, 501 (c)(3) organization that supports efforts in the United States to improve the national academic dropout rate and increase college accessibility by partnering with established public school systems. SYF is headquartered in Indianapolis, Indiana. Its current CEO is J. Michael Durnil, Ph.D.»
[3] «Stanford Shopping Center | Stanford Shopping Center is an upscale open air shopping mall located on Route 82 (El Camino Real) at Sand Hill Road in Palo Alto, California. It is on the campus of Stanford University although the university only owns the land and not the actual buildings or stores. Also, unlike the rest of the campus, the shopping center and the neighboring Stanford University Medical Center are part of the city of Palo Alto, not the census-designated place (CDP) of Stanford, California. The shopping center buildings are owned by Simon Property Group, which manages the property and leases the land from the university.»

signature.instructions: Given the fields `question` and `trajectory`, reason through the information gathered to produce a coherent `answer` based on the accumulated knowledge and interactions. Ensure that the final output reflects the logical steps taken to arrive at the conclusion.

fields.prefix: Reasoning: Let's think step by step in order to
